architecture.summary: C3V extends implicit in-context learning by constructing separate context vectors for each class instead of a single task-level vector. During inference, class-conditional context vectors are computed from demonstration examples and additively injected into the model's residual stream in a single forward pass, enabling class-aware contextual modulation without training the backbone LLM.

biblio.title: Beyond Task-Level Context: Class-Conditional Context Vectors for Implicit In-Context Learning

claims.statement: Class-conditional context vectors explicitly model class-specific contextual information by constructing separate context vectors for each class, without relying on explicit prompts
C3V compresses demonstration examples into class-conditional context vectors and achieves few-shot-level performance at near zero-shot inference cost
C3V consistently outperforms task-level context vector baselines
C3V achieves higher average accuracy than conventional few-shot learning on most models
